description: Template rendering, regex, and JSONPath transforms.

tools.description: Render a Jinja2 template using a set of variables. Use to build prompts, message bodies, or request payloads from flow data.

schema.name: template
schema.description: The Jinja2 template text, e.g. 'Hi {{ name }}, you have {{ count }} items.' Ignored when source_uri is provided.

schema.name: variables
schema.description: Variables to render with, as a JSON object.

schema.name: source_uri
schema.description: Optional URI to read the template body from. Overrides 'template' when set. Formats: master:<catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path>, workspace:/<path>, or <catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path>.

schema.name: dest_uri
schema.description: Optional URI to write the rendered text to. Same URI formats as source_uri. The rendered text is still returned in the response envelope.

_confDescriptions.autoescape: Jinja2 autoescape toggle. true escapes HTML-sensitive chars in output; false (default) leaves them as-is.
_confDescriptions.strict: If true, undefined template variables raise an error. If false (default), they render as empty strings.
_confDescriptions.source_uri: Optional input URI for the template body. Accepts master:<catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path>, workspace:/<path>, or the bare <catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path> alias. When set, overrides the inline 'template' parameter.
_confDescriptions.dest_uri: Optional output URI for the rendered text. Accepts the same URI formats as source_uri. Writing the destination does not suppress the rendered text in the response envelope.

tools.description: Extract all matches of a regex from text, or replace matches with a substitution. Use for pulling structured fragments (emails, ids, codes) out of free text or cleaning text.

schema.name: text
schema.description: The input text. Ignored when source_uri is provided.

schema.name: mode
schema.description: Regex operation: 'match' (anchored at start), 'search' (first match anywhere), 'findall' (all matches, default), 'sub' (replace), 'split' (split on pattern). Legacy aliases 'extract'->'findall' and 'replace'->'sub' still work.

schema.name: replacement
schema.description: Replacement string when mode is 'sub'. Supports backrefs like \1.

schema.name: source_uri
schema.description: Optional URI to read the haystack text from. Overrides 'text' when set. Formats: master:<catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path>, workspace:/<path>, or <catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path>.

schema.name: dest_uri
schema.description: Optional URI to write the match results to as JSON. Same URI formats as source_uri. Results are still returned in the response envelope.

_confDescriptions.multiline: Multiline mode (re.MULTILINE): ^ and $ match at line boundaries. Default false.
_confDescriptions.max_matches: Cap on number of matches returned by findall/split to prevent runaway output. Default 500.
_confDescriptions.source_uri: Optional input URI for the haystack text. Accepts master:<catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path>, workspace:/<path>, or the bare <catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path> alias. When set, overrides the inline 'text' parameter.
_confDescriptions.dest_uri: Optional output URI for the match results (serialized as pretty JSON). Accepts the same URI formats as source_uri. Writing the destination does not suppress results in the response envelope.

tools.description: Pull values out of JSON with a JSONPath expression, or reshape JSON into a new object using a field->path mapping. Use to extract the relevant fields from an API response.

schema.name: data
schema.description: The JSON to read from (object/array or JSON string). Ignored when source_uri is provided.

schema.name: path
schema.description: A single JSONPath expression, e.g. '$.items[*].id'. Use this OR mapping.

schema.name: mapping
schema.description: A JSON object of {output_field: jsonpath} to build a new shape. Use this OR path.

schema.name: source_uri
schema.description: Optional URI to read the input JSON from. Overrides 'data' when set. Formats: master:<catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path>, workspace:/<path>, or <catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path>.

schema.name: dest_uri
schema.description: Optional URI to write the transformed JSON output to. Same URI formats as source_uri. The output is still returned in the response envelope.

_confDescriptions.max_input_bytes: Cap on input JSON size in bytes; oversized input is rejected. Default 1048576 (1 MiB).
_confDescriptions.max_results: Cap on number of JSONPath matches returned to prevent runaway output. Default 1000.
_confDescriptions.source_uri: Optional input URI for the JSON document. Accepts master:<catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path>, workspace:/<path>, or the bare <catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path> alias. When set, overrides the inline 'data' parameter.
_confDescriptions.dest_uri: Optional output URI for the transformed JSON (pretty-printed). Accepts the same URI formats as source_uri. Writing the destination does not suppress the result in the response envelope.
